headline: MA63 Special Committee Has Met Only 8 Times in 4 Years
context: The Special Cabinet Committee on MA63, formed in 2020 to resolve outstanding federal-state issues, has met only 8 times in 4 years and resolved 6 of 21 identified issues.

cards.big: The MA63 Special Committee was supposed to resolve 21 outstanding issues.
cards.sub: In 4 years: 8 meetings, 6 issues resolved, 15 pending. At this rate, resolution will take 10 more years.

cards.big: Oil royalty calculation methodology remains unresolved since 1974.
cards.sub: Sabah and Sarawak claim RM60B in underpaid royalties under the Petroleum Development Act.

cards.big: Immigration autonomy — a core MA63 right — is partially implemented.
cards.sub: Sabah and Sarawak control immigration from Peninsular Malaysia but not automated gate systems.

cards.big: 64% of Sabahans and 71% of Sarawakians feel MA63 rights are not respected.
cards.sub: A 2025 survey found deepening dissatisfaction with the pace of devolution.

cards.big: 6 of 21 issues resolved in 4 years. The committee exists. Progress barely does.
MA63 is not a wish list. It is the constitutional basis for Sabah and Sarawak joining Malaysia. Treating it as a negotiation rather than an obligation undermines the federation itself.
